title: docker-compose can't pull locally built images
body:
### Issue Description

When running `podman compose up` with `docker-compose` as the compose engine, I cannot use a local image reference in the service definition. `docker-compose` reports that the image is not found.

If I use the image id then it works.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman build .`
2. `podman tag <id> <name>:<tag>`
3. `podman images` shows `localhost/<name>:<tag>`
4. Edit `docker-compose.yaml` to include the image `localhost/<name>:<tag>`
5.  `podman compose up`

### Describe the results you received

`podman compose` reports `Error response from daemon: no such image: localhost/<name>:<tag> image not known`

### Describe the results you expected

Image to be loaded by the engine

comments.body:
~~docker-compose will always resolve to docker.io for images referenced by short names.  So `localhost/foo:bar` will be turned into `docker.io/localhost/foo:bar` by docker-compose (and Docker REST API).~~

~~In your workflow you are mixing Podman's and Docker's way of referencing short names.  A quick work around for you would be to add a domain in step two and use that in the compose file: `podman tag <id> localhost.com/foo:bar`~~

~~@mtrmac maybe `NormalizeToDockerHub` should consider localhost/ ?~~

Hi, thanks for the quick reply - I tried the new tag and it has the same result.

```
Error response from daemon: no such image: localhost.com/foo:bar: image not known
```
Does `podman images localhost.com/foo:bar` show the image?

```
localhost.com/foo    bar                 485e15544204  3 days ago     3.24 GB
localhost/foo   bar                 485e15544204  3 days ago     3.24 GB
```

Ah, scratch what I wrote before.  Podman's REST API is smart enough to resolve to `localhost/` even for the Docker endpoints.

I do not know what's going, sorry.  @benoitf have you seen this with compose already?
can you give a sample compose file so we have an exact reproducer ?

Hmm, I can't reproduce this today... `podman tag <id> <name>:<tag>` is now working fine in my `docker-compose.yml`.

The difference is that I am not on the corporate network. I'll try again on Monday, just in case there's a difference. If not, I'll close this!
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Please reopen if more information becomes available.
